title: pedicularis gyroflexa subsp. cenisia
intro: Pedicularis gyroflexa subsp. cenisia, also known as Cenisian Lousewort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, which is a type of parasitic plant that obtains its nutrients by drawing them from the roots of other plants. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which make it useful in treating various health issues. The medicinal properties of Pedicularis gyroflexa subsp. cenisia are believed to provide relief from respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as digestive issues and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
